topic: desert camping trip last weekend — loved it

turns.content: Okay I HAVE to tell you about last weekend because it was one of the most incredible experiences I've had since moving to Dubai haha
I'm all ears! What happened last weekend?

So a group of us from the clinic went desert camping!! Like actual overnight camping in the dunes, not the glamping tourist thing. We drove out Friday afternoon, set up tents, made a fire. Gente, the stars out there — ai meu Deus, I have never seen so many stars in my life. In the city you forget they exist!

That sounds amazing. The lack of light pollution out in the desert makes the sky completely different — people are often stunned by how many stars are actually visible. How many of you went, and was it your first time camping in the desert?

First time in the desert!! I've done beach camping in Brazil a hundred times, but the desert is a totally different vibe. There were eight of us. We brought coolers with food, someone had a portable grill. We made kebabs and grilled vegetables and sat around the fire talking until like 2am. The silence out there is unreal — no traffic, no construction, nothing. Just wind and sand.

There's something restorative about that kind of silence, especially when you're used to city noise all day. The contrast between Dubai's urban intensity and the emptiness of the desert just an hour away is striking. How did you handle the heat? June in the desert must have been intense, even at night.

So the daytime was BRUTAL, like 45 degrees, we basically hid under a shade structure and just drank water nonstop. But the night was actually really pleasant! Maybe 30 degrees? With the breeze it felt nice. I slept on top of my sleeping bag, not inside it haha. And waking up at sunrise in the dunes — nossa, the colors. The sand turns gold and pink. I took like 200 photos.

Desert sunrises are supposed to be spectacular — the way the light hits the dunes creates those layered color shifts that are hard to replicate anywhere else. Smart to plan most of the activity around evening and early morning. Did you do any dune activities — sandboarding, dune bashing, that sort of thing?

We tried sandboarding!! I was TERRIBLE at it haha. I kept falling and getting sand absolutely everywhere — in my ears, my hair, places sand should not be. But it was so fun. One of my colleagues is really into it and he was doing these smooth runs down the dunes while the rest of us just tumbled like potatoes rolling downhill.

The image of tumbling down a sand dune like a potato is pretty vivid. At least sand is a forgiving surface to fall on compared to, say, a snowboard slope. Did the sandboarding leave you sore the next day, or were you too energized to notice?

Oh my quads were DESTROYED for two days! Climbing back up the dunes after each run — gente, walking uphill in deep sand is the hardest leg workout I've ever done. My patients always ask me for good quad exercises and honestly I should just tell them to go walk up sand dunes for an hour haha. Best lower body workout ever, no equipment needed!

Walking uphill in soft sand is genuinely brutal — your stabilizer muscles work overtime because the surface keeps shifting under every step. It's one of those things that no gym exercise quite replicates because the instability is so constant. It sounds like the kind of trip that reminds you why you live somewhere, even when the daily routine gets monotonous.

That's EXACTLY it, querida. Sometimes I get so caught up in work and missing Brazil that I forget — I live in this wild, beautiful place. The desert is right there! And it's like another planet. I told everyone we have to make it a monthly thing. Maybe not in July and August because we would literally melt haha, but starting again in October for sure.

October through March would be the sweet spot — cool enough at night to actually enjoy camping, and the daytime would be manageable too. Having a regular adventure like that with your colleagues could be a really nice anchor outside of work. Do you think the group will actually commit to making it recurring?

I think so! Everyone was so into it. We already have a group chat called 'Desert Crew' haha. Someone suggested we try different spots each time — there are areas with more rocky terrain, some with bigger dunes, some near the mountains toward Hatta. I love that idea. I want to explore ALL of it.

The Hatta area would be a great change of scenery — it's more mountainous with wadis and rock pools, very different from the open sand dunes. Rotating locations would keep it fresh and give everyone a reason to keep showing up. It sounds like this trip was exactly the kind of reset you needed. I hope the Desert Crew stays strong once the cooler months arrive.

It really was a reset. I came back to work on Sunday feeling so recharged. Like my brain had been rinsed clean by the silence and the stars haha. Dubai surprised me with this one — almost six years here and I'm still discovering things. Okay, enough gushing, but I just needed to share because it was THAT good!

It's clear that it made an impression. Those unexpected discoveries are often the best ones — you didn't know you needed a night in the desert until you had one. Enjoy the quad soreness while it lasts, and start scouting those Hatta campsites for the fall.
